# whatsmeow-node

> WhatsApp for Node.js — powered by the most battle-tested WhatsApp Web library

whatsmeow-node is a fully typed Node.js client for WhatsApp Web. It wraps [whatsmeow](https://github.com/tulir/whatsmeow), the Go library that powers the Mautrix WhatsApp bridge (used by thousands of Matrix users daily), and exposes it as a native npm package with 100 typed methods.

The Go binary communicates with Node.js via JSON-line IPC over stdin/stdout. No native compilation required — prebuilt binaries are provided for macOS, Linux, and Windows (x64 and arm64).

## Key Features

- Battle-tested upstream: backed by whatsmeow, the most reliable WhatsApp Web library
- Lightweight: 10–20 MB prebuilt binary, no Chromium or browser dependency
- Fully typed: 100 async methods with TypeScript interfaces
- Multi-platform: macOS, Linux, Windows (x64, arm64, musl)
- Persistent sessions: SQLite (dev) or PostgreSQL (prod)

## Documentation

- [Why whatsmeow-node](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/intro): Problem statement, design philosophy, comparison with Baileys and whatsapp-web.js
- [Installation](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/installation): Node.js 18+ requirement, npm install, platform support, store options
- [Getting Started](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/getting-started): Quick start, client options, sending messages, media, contacts, cleanup
- [Connection Lifecycle](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/connection-lifecycle): Flow diagram, key events, resilient connection pattern
- [Rate Limiting](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/rate-limiting): Observed thresholds, safe sending patterns, backoff strategies
- [Examples](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/examples): Runnable example scripts (pair, send, smoke test)
- [API Overview](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/api/overview): Full method reference across 18 categories (messaging, media, groups, newsletters, privacy, and more)
- [Events](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/api/events): All emitted events (connection, message, presence, group, media, call, history sync)
- [Errors](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/api/errors): Error classes, common error codes, method-specific error codes
- [Common Issues](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/troubleshooting/common-issues): QR issues, ERR_NOT_INIT, send failures, timeouts, proto field naming
- [Acknowledgments](https://nicastelo.github.io/whatsmeow-node/docs/acknowledgments): Upstream attribution, licensing (MIT / MPL-2.0)

## Quick Start

```typescript
import { createClient } from '@whatsmeow-node/whatsmeow-node';

const client = createClient({ store: 'session.db' });
await client.init();
const qr = await client.getQRChannel();
await client.connect();
// Scan QR code, then send a message:
await client.sendMessage('1234567890@s.whatsapp.net', { conversation: 'Hello!' });
```

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@whatsmeow-node/whatsmeow-node
- GitHub: https://github.com/nicastelo/whatsmeow-node
- Upstream (whatsmeow): https://github.com/tulir/whatsmeow
